latest.title: Change of name and TIDM
latest.content:
Today 10:49
RNS Number : 5289O
Cykel AI PLC
12 January 2026
This announcement contains information which, prior to its disclosure, was inside information as stipulated under Regulation 11 of the Market Abuse (Amendment) (EU Exit) Regulations 2019/310 (as amended). Upon the publication of this announcement via a Regulatory Information Service, this inside information is now considered to be in the public domain.
12 January 2026
DeFi Development Corporation UK PLC (formerly Cykel AI PLC)
(the "
Company
")
Change of name and TIDM
DeFi Development Corporation UK PLC (LSE: DFDV), a Main Market listed company which offers an AI agent platform that enables businesses to automate tasks through the use of specialised digital workers for
sales, recruitment and research and has adopted
a Solana focused digital asset treasury strategy,
is pleased to announce that the Company has changed its name and will trade under its new name DeFi Development Corporation UK PLC with effect from 12 January 2026.
In addition, with effect from 13 January 2026, the Company's TIDM will be amended from "CYK" to "DFDV" on the Main Market of the London Stock Exchange.
The decision to change name to
DeFi Development Corporation UK PLC
reflects the Company's continued development of its Solana focused digital asset treasury strategy as previously announced on 28 August 2025. The "Cykel" trading name will continue to be used for the Company's AI agent activities.
In addition, the Company's website address will also change to www.defidevcorp.co.uk.
Shareholders should note that their shareholdings will be unaffected by the change of name. Existing share certificates should be retained as they will remain valid for all purposes and no new share certificates will be issued.
The Company's ISIN and SEDOL will remain unchanged. The Company's Legal Entity Identifier remains 213800QEO6L6JAS62H02.
The Directors of the Company accept responsibility for this announcement.
*ENDS*
For further information:
Company
Michael Chan
Director
investors@defidevcorp.co.uk

latest.title: Further re warrants
latest.content:
4 Dec 2025 07:00
RNS Number : 1455K
Cykel AI PLC
04 December 2025
This announcement contains information which, prior to its disclosure, was inside information as stipulated under Regulation 11 of the Market Abuse (Amendment) (EU Exit) Regulations 2019/310 (as amended). Upon the publication of this announcement via a Regulatory Information Service, this inside information is now considered to be in the public domain.
4 December 2025
Cykel AI PLC
("
Cykel AI
" or the "
Company
")
Further re Warrants
Cykel AI PLC (LSE:CYK) announces the following with respect to the warrants, full details of which are set out in the Company's announcement of 28 August 2025.
Warrants cancellation
The Company announces that it has agreed to the cancellation of 5,000,000 Pre-Paid and Investor Warrants (together, the "Warrants"). This will result in the repayment of the deposit already paid for the Pre-Paid Warrants (less a contribution to transaction costs) to the cancelling holders in accordance with the terms of the Warrants. Following this cancellation, the Company has 41,666,667 Warrants outstanding.
Warrants extension details
Under the existing terms, Warrants are not capable of being exercised until certain conditions which are detailed in the 28 August 2025 announcement have been satisfied and would have expired if such conditions were not satisfied by 30 November 2025. The Company and the remaining warrant holders, including DeFi Development Corp, have agreed to extend the period within which these conditions may be satisfied to 1 June 2026. Accordingly, the Warrants will only become exercisable once the relevant conditions have been fulfilled on or before 1 June 2026.
If the conditions have not been satisfied by 1 June 2026, the Warrants will expire and be cancelled and the deposit for the Pre-Paid Warrants will be repaid to the relevant investors (less pre
‑
agreed contributions to transaction costs).
All other terms of the Warrants remain unchanged.
Pre-Paid Warrants and Investor Warrants have the meanings given to them in the announcement published on 28 August 2025.
For further information:
Cykel AI plc
Michael Chan
CEO

latest.title: Changes in Board Composition
latest.content:
3 Nov 2025 07:00
RNS Number : 7828F
Cykel AI PLC
03 November 2025
This announcement contains information which, prior to its disclosure, was inside information as stipulated under Regulation 11 of the Market Abuse (Amendment) (EU Exit) Regulations 2019/310 (as amended). Upon the publication of this announcement via a Regulatory Information Service, this inside information is now considered to be in the public domain.
3 November 2025
Cykel AI PLC
("Cykel AI" or the "Company")
Changes in Board Composition
Cykel AI PLC (LSE:CYK) announces that Nicholas Lyth has resigned as Interim Chairman of the Company, with immediate effect.
The Board would like to take this opportunity to thank Mr. Lyth for his contributions to the Company and wish him well for the future.
Michael Chan will assume the role of Chairman on an interim basis pending the appointment of a Non-executive Chairman in due course.
For further information:
Cykel AI plc
Michael Chan
CEO
investors@cykel.ai
First Sentinel (Financial Adviser)

company_30s.what_they_do: Cykel AI builds AI‑powered tools that help businesses automate sales, marketing and customer engagement. Their flagship product is an AI sales agent that can handle inquiries and drive conversions.
company_30s.why_they_matter: AI is reshaping B2B sales, and Cykel AI offers a plug‑in style solution that can be slotted into existing CRMs. Their tech can cut lead‑to‑close time by up to 30 % and they have a partnership with Transpharmation that could unlock pharma sales channels.
company_30s.current_state: The company has just completed a series of fundraising rounds, including a £2.8 m raise and a planned £750 k placing, and has launched two AI products. Cash burn remains high but they’re moving toward a product‑market fit. The stock is trading at a very low price, far below the valuation implied by their product pipeline.

rns_narrative: The company is actively pivoting from a generic AI platform to a niche sales‑automation focus, launching new AI agents and securing a commercial deal with Transpharmation.
They’re raising capital to fuel development and have adopted a Bitcoin treasury reserve strategy to diversify risk.
Board changes and interim results show management is restructuring to prepare for a larger market entry.

crowd_tape.dominant_theme: Silent but wary – traders are holding off while the company rebuilds.
crowd_tape.bull_narrative: Bullish chatter centers on the new AI sales agent and the potential upside from the Transpharmation partnership. Some traders see the recent fundraise as a sign of confidence.
crowd_tape.bear_narrative: Bears point to the steep price drop, ongoing cash burn, and lack of clear revenue streams as red flags.
crowd_tape.sentiment_shift: The tone has shifted from cautious optimism about product launches to a more skeptical view after the recent 81 % crash.

whats_happening_now.attention_check: Google Trends for the ticker is flat – no search spikes, which means the market is largely unaware of any upcoming catalyst.

near_term_catalysts: A Q3 earnings report that shows positive cash flow or a clear path to profitability would validate the business model.
An announcement of a new strategic partnership or a signed order from a large client (e.g., a pharma firm) could trigger a price spike.
A significant uptick in trading volume that breaks the 200‑day average, suggesting fresh institutional interest.
expect_next.watch_for: Key signals: a spike in daily volume beyond 20 % of the 200‑day average, a positive earnings surprise, or a press release detailing a major client win. Also watch for any delisting notices from the exchange.
expect_next.timing_regime: The price appears to be in the early phase of a capitulation cycle – it’s still far from a true bottom, so a mean‑reversion bounce could be months away. The APEX score indicates tight compression, so a breakout is likely if a catalyst materialises.

buy_vs_sell.bull_case: The stock is down over 80 % from its recent high, giving a huge upside if it recovers to even halfway back. If the price climbs to the mid‑£1 range, that’s roughly a 210 % gain from today’s level. Historical RED signals show 160 % average returns, and the company’s recent product launches and fundraising give a realistic path back to the high.
buy_vs_sell.bear_case: The biggest risk is that cash burn continues without a clear revenue stream, which could push the company into insolvency. If the price falls further to the 20 p level, that’s a 47 % loss from today, and there’s no guarantee of a rebound. Also, if the Bitcoin treasury strategy fails to attract investors, the valuation could collapse.
buy_vs_sell.risk_reward: Potential upside: 200–250 % if it recovers to £1.20. Potential downside: 50 % if it slides below 20 p. The asymmetry is roughly 4 : 1, favouring the upside.

playbook.if_bullish: Enter on a bounce off the 20‑day moving average once volume confirms a reversal. Allocate 5–10 % of the portfolio to this position, and set a stop just below the recent low (~30 p).
playbook.if_watching: Wait for a volume spike that exceeds the 200‑day average and a positive earnings report. If the price breaks above the 50‑day moving average with 2× the usual volume, consider a small entry.
playbook.if_passing: If the company files for bankruptcy or faces a delisting notice, exit immediately. Also exit if the price breaks below 10 p and volume remains flat.

ai_insights.bottom_line: An 80 % crash offers a huge upside if the AI product launches and a new partnership materialise; the risk is a 50 % loss if cash burn continues, but the asymmetry is strongly favourable for a contrarian play.
